dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6951 IH: Stop Unfair Medicaid Recoveries Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Schakowsky (for herself, Ms. Barragán, Ms. Castor of Florida, Mrs. Cherfilus-McCormick, Mr. Cohen, Mr. Doggett, Ms. Garcia of Texas, Mr. Goldman of New York, Ms. Norton, Ms. Kelly of Illinois, Ms. Matsui, Ms. Ocasio-Cortez, Ms. Omar, Ms. Pingree, Mr. Quigley, Mrs. Ramirez, Ms. Strickland, Mr. Tonko, Mrs. Trahan, and Ms. Wasserman Schultz) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend title XIX of the Social Security Act to repeal the requirement that States establish a Medicaid Estate Recovery Program and to limit the circumstances in which a State may place a lien on a Medicaid beneficiary’s property.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Stop Unfair Medicaid Recoveries Act.

section.header: Liens, adjustments, and recoveries for medical assistance

subsection.text: Section 1917(a) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396p(a)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in the matter preceding subparagraph (A), by striking plan, except— and inserting plan, except, subject to paragraph (4)—; and
in subparagraph (B), by striking in the case of and inserting with respect to liens imposed before the date of the enactment of the Stop Unfair Medicaid Recoveries Act, in the case of; and

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following:
Notwithstanding any preceding provision of this subsection, not later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this paragraph, a State shall—

subparagraph.text: withdraw any lien imposed under paragraph (1)(B) that is in effect as of such date; and
notify each individual (or legal representative of such individual (or of such individual’s estate)) subject to such a lien so withdrawn of the withdrawal of such lien.

subsection.text: Section 1917(b) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396p(b)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (1), by striking except that and inserting except that, subject to paragraph (6),; and
by adding at the end the following:

Notwithstanding any preceding provision of this subsection, no adjustment or recovery of any medical assistance correctly paid on behalf of an individual under the State plan may be initiated, maintained, or collected on or after the date of the enactment of this paragraph. Not later than 90 days after such date, a State shall—

subparagraph.text: withdraw any lien in effect as of such date with respect to such medical assistance correctly paid; and

notify each individual (or legal representative of such individual (or of such individual’s estate)) subject to such a lien so withdrawn of the withdrawal of such lien and the prohibition on adjustment or recovery under this paragraph.
